html.zh: 在深度学习主导 AI 之前，搜索是这个领域的核心隐喻之一。霍兰德 将生物适应引入计算，使研究人工种群中的学习与优化成为可能。
html.en: Before deep learning dominated AI, search was one of the field’s central metaphors. Holland brought biological adaptation into computation, making it possible to study learning and optimization in artificial populations.

html.zh: 遗传算法不是直接改进单个解，而是反复评分、选择、重组和变异候选解，让一个种群逐步演化。
html.en: A genetic algorithm does not improve one solution directly. It evolves a population by repeatedly scoring, selecting, recombining, and mutating candidates.

html.zh: 专家通常把遗传算法视为困难设计空间中的有用搜索启发式方法，而不是通用智能理论。它们的长期影响在于基于种群的探索，适用于没有梯度、目标函数棘手或候选设计需要演化的任务。
html.en: Experts generally treat genetic algorithms as useful search heuristics for difficult design spaces, not as a general theory of intelligence. Their long-term legacy is population-based exploration for problems where gradients are unavailable, objectives are awkward, or candidate designs must be evolved.

notes.zh: 由 legacy AI100 数据批量生成的 variant。
notes.en: Variant generated from legacy AI100 data in a batch migration.

workTitle.en: Adaptation in Natural and Artificial Systems
workTitle.zh: 自然系统与人工系统中的适应

quotePage.zh: BenchCouncil AI100：搜索与优化 / 遗传算法

quoteAttribution.en: Adaptation in Natural and Artificial Systems, John H. Holland, University of Michigan Press / MIT Press edition, 1975
quoteAttribution.zh: 《自然系统与人工系统中的适应》, 约翰·H·霍兰德, 密歇根大学出版社 / 麻省理工学院出版社版本, 1975

material.en: Adaptation in Natural and Artificial Systems

text.en: The algorithm keeps many candidates at once, so search can explore multiple regions instead of following one path.
text.zh: 算法同时保留多个候选解，因此可以探索多个区域，而不是只沿一条路径前进。

text.en: Parts of successful candidates are recombined, allowing useful partial structures to move into new solutions.
text.zh: 成功候选解的片段会被重组，使有用的局部结构进入新解。

text.en: Random changes preserve diversity and let the population escape premature convergence.
text.zh: 随机变化保持多样性，并帮助种群避免过早收敛。

title.en: Adaptation in Natural and Artificial Systems
title.zh: 自然系统与人工系统中的适应
